title: rosa tomentosa subvar. multiglandulosa
intro: Rosa tomentosa subvar. multiglandulosa, also known as the hairy rose or Rosa tomentosa var. multiglandulosa, is a species of rose that is native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Rosa tomentosa subvar. multiglandulosa include reducing inflammation and pain, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system, while also exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Rosa tomentosa subvar. multiglandulosa can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
